retry_reason: If the channel lookup fails (channel not found), the object is unknown or already gone; retrying will not make it appear.
contract_summary: This function handles a VCU destroy channel response. It looks up the channel by ID, completes the channel's completion to wake up any waiters, and then releases the reference acquired during the lookup. It is a release operation on the channel's refcount after handling the destroy notification. The function does not transfer ownership of the channel to the caller; the channel is only used internally. On lookup failure, it returns -EINVAL and no reference is taken. After successful return, the channel's refcount has been decremented and the object may be freed.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to acquire a reference to a channel (via allegro_ref_get_channel_by_channel_id), handle the destroy response (complete a completion and log), and release the reference (via allegro_ref_put_channel). The refcount operations are the central mechanism; the completion and logging are ancillary but part of the destroy handling.
judge.retry_reason: If the channel lookup fails (channel not found), the object is unknown or already gone; retrying will not make it appear.
judge.contract_summary: This function handles a VCU destroy channel response. It looks up the channel by ID, completes the channel's completion to wake up any waiters, and then releases the reference acquired during the lookup. check.reason: The get operation is incorrectly assigned to the return value of allegro_handle_destroy_channel (int), not to the actual channel object. The put operation is incorrectly assigned to parameter 1 (msg) instead of the channel object. Both location/index mappings are inconsistent with the function's interface, so the operations are invalid.

reevaluate.reason: The function gets a channel reference via allegro_ref_get_channel_by_channel_id and puts it after use, but the critical variable (channel) is not passed to the caller via return or parameters; it is used internally only. Therefore the function does not act as a reference-count wrapper/helper that provides a referenced object to the caller. The candidate operations' location/index mappings are invalid: get cannot be on the int return value, and put cannot be on the msg parameter. Hence no valid operations.

final.retry_reason: If the channel lookup fails (channel not found), the object is unknown or already gone; retrying will not make it appear.
